Question 1

Correct
Mark 1.00 out of 1.00

Question text

Given the following class definition, which of the following correctly sets the name of the person to "John"?
Question 1 Answer
a.
Person p = new Person(); p.setName("John");
b.
Person p = new Person(); p.name = "John";
c.
Person p = new Person(); p.name("John");
d.
Person p = new Person(); p.getName("John");

Feedback

The correct answer is: Person p = new Person(); p.setName("John");
Correct
Marks for this submission: 1.00/1.00.

Question 2

Correct
Mark 1.00 out of 1.00

Question text

What is a constructor in Java?
Question 2 Answer
a.
A method that is called automatically when an object is destroyed
b.
A method that must be explicitly called to initialize objects
c.
A method that performs arithmetic operations
d.
A special method used to initialize objects

Feedback

The correct answer is: A special method used to initialize objects
Correct
Marks for this submission: 1.00/1.00.

Question 3

Correct
Mark 1.00 out of 1.00

Question text

What is the purpose of a class in Java?
Question 3 Answer
a.
To perform arithmetic operations
b.
To define a blueprint for objects
c.
To define methods only
d.
To serve as a placeholder for variables

Feedback

The correct answer is: To define a blueprint for objects
Correct
Marks for this submission: 1.00/1.00.

Question text

Which access modifier allows a class attribute or method to be accessible from any other class?
Question 4 Answer
a.
public
b.
private
c.
default
d.
protected

Feedback

The correct answer is: public
Correct

Correct

Question text

Which of the following statements correctly creates an instance of a class named Student?
Question 5 Answer
a.
Student student = Student();
b.
Student student;
c.
Student student = new Student;
d.
Student student = new Student();

Feedback

The correct answer is: Student student = new Student();
Correct
